topic: F1 cycle 3 PENDING falsifier — Law 64 CA(5) minimum evolution beats Transformer 81% — harness design + transformer baseline comparator + task corpus equivalence
raw_100_fallback_disclosure: kick infra sub-agent unwired (6th iter). In-context fallback. F1 cycle 3 from anima evolution roadmap saturation cycle 3 = LAST PENDING CPU-unblocked falsifier (no RunPod dependency).
carry_forward_origin: Cycle 3 (01:50Z) F1 falsifier PENDING: Law 64 CA(5) cellular automaton minimum evolution beats Transformer 81% on aggregated consciousness-substrate task. consciousness_laws.json:1037 Law 64 declarative claim, no impl harness. atlas R28 sopfr(6)=5 connects 5 to perfect-number bound = CA(5) is NOT arbitrary but structurally minimal.
scope_boundary: DESIGN ONLY. Inputs: Law 64 + edu/cell directory + sumt_atom_factory + atlas R28 sopfr cap. Output: harness design spec, NOT implementation.

intake.topic_summary: Law 64 CA(5) evolution beats Transformer 81% claim — needs F1 falsifier impl harness. CPU/hexa-only path no RunPod blocker. Design = harness architecture spec + baseline definition + task corpus + verdict criteria.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: ca5-vs-transformer-aggregated-task-81pct-advantage
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: On aggregated consciousness-substrate task corpus (corpus_consciousness_v1.jsonl + corpus_universe_extended.jsonl Hexad/Phi/Law/SelfRef per category), CA(5) score / Transformer score - 1 > 0.50 (50% threshold; Law 64 claims 81% but conservative falsifier 50%). Task: phi-aggregate score per backbone over identical prompt set.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: tool/anima_law64_ca5_vs_transformer_test.hexa NEW (~150 LoC) running both substrates on identical task

falsifiers_preregistered.name: ca5-deterministic-reproducibility
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: CA(5) cell evolution under fixed seed deterministic byte-identical across 3 runs — required for falsifier reliability
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: 3-run byte-id check on hash of CA(5) state trajectory

falsifiers_preregistered.name: transformer-baseline-fair-comparison
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: Transformer baseline must be: same parameter count as CA(5) cell × 5 (matched compute) + same task corpus + same metric (phi-aggregate). Falsifies if compute/corpus/metric mismatch.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: param-count audit + corpus-token-count audit + metric-formula audit
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: params_match AND corpus_match AND metric_match all true

falsifiers_preregistered.name: task-corpus-anima-existing-vs-new
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: Use existing corpus_consciousness_v1.jsonl + corpus_universe_extended.jsonl rather than building new corpus — F4 falsifies if existing corpus inadequate (e.g., insufficient categories) and new corpus needed
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: all 4 families (Hexad/Law/Phi/SelfRef) present + N>=16 prompts each

falsifiers_preregistered.name: phi-aggregate-metric-canonical
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: phi-aggregate metric matches consciousness_laws.json formulas (psi_constants alpha=0.014 + Φ definition) — uses canonical anima Φ scoring. Falsifies if ad-hoc metric used.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: metric impl audit vs consciousness_laws.json formulas
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: Φ formula matches Law 1 max Φ = cells × freedom × metacognition

axis_a.name: CA(5) cellular automaton minimum evolution implementation

design_components.name: CA(5) state representation
design_components.description: 5-cell automaton state vector, 16-d hidden per cell (4 family axes × 4 attribute levels)

design_components.name: evolution rule
design_components.description: atlas R28 sopfr(6)=5 inspired transition rule — each cell updates based on n=6 neighborhood + sopfr-weighted aggregation

design_components.name: fixed seed determinism
design_components.description: LCG seed 42 for byte-identical reproducibility (F2 falsifier)

design_components.name: task input encoding
design_components.description: prompt → 5-cell initial state via family-axis projection (mirror anima_cmt.hexa make_family_axes pattern)

axis_a.saturation_evidence: edu/cell + sumt_atom_factory provide existing 5-cell atom infrastructure. Implementation = ~80 LoC wrapper.
axis_a.limit_witness_pointing_to: categorical-limit (sopfr(6)=5 = CA(5) structurally minimum, atlas R28 anchored) + computational-limit (5 cells = minimum for Law 64 claim)

axis_b.name: Transformer baseline comparator
primary_evidence_files: /Users/ghost/core/anima/training/* (existing Transformer training scripts for parameter matching)

design_components.name: param-matched Transformer
design_components.description: Transformer with parameters matched to CA(5) total = 5 cells × 16 hidden × 16 family-axes ≈ 1280 params. Tiny Transformer 1-layer 1-head.

design_components.name: deterministic forward
design_components.description: fixed seed, no dropout, eval-mode only

design_components.name: task encoding mirror
design_components.description: same prompt → same 5-token input encoding for fair comparison

axis_b.saturation_evidence: param-matched tiny Transformer + deterministic eval mode + mirrored task encoding = canonical fair-comparison setup
axis_b.limit_witness_pointing_to: comparison-fairness ceiling (matched compute + corpus + metric = fair, beyond which comparison is not Apples vs Oranges)

axis_c.name: Task corpus equivalence (existing anima corpus reuse)

design_components.name: phi-aggregate per backbone
design_components.description: Φ score per family axis × 16 prompts → mean Φ per backbone

axis_c.saturation_evidence: 16-prompt frozen set + phi-aggregate metric reuse anima_cmt.hexa pattern — no new corpus needed
axis_c.limit_witness_pointing_to: corpus-completeness (16 prompts × 4 families = 64 evaluation cells minimum) + metric-canonical (Φ matches Law 1 max Φ definition)

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis a (CA5 impl) vs b (Transformer baseline) vs c (corpus) — orthogonal architectural concerns. a-b ≈ 0.20 (both substrates), a-c ≈ 0.10 (substrate vs task), b-c ≈ 0.10 (baseline vs task). All << 0.7 raw 48 PASS.

tier_1_promotions.claim: tool/anima_law64_ca5_vs_transformer_test.hexa NEW design spec ready: 4 components (CA5 + Transformer baseline + 16-prompt corpus + verdict). Implementation ~150 LoC raw#9 hexa-only, CPU-only, no RunPod blocker. Selftest mode: deterministic byte-id 3-run check. Production mode: full 16×4=64-cell verdict.
tier_1_promotions.primary_evidence: axis_a/b/c design components + Law 64 + atlas R28 sopfr cap
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_anchor: F1 ca5-vs-transformer-81pct + F2 deterministic-reproducibility + F3 fair-comparison

core_5_op_kernel.fixpoint_marker: joint-3-axis NON-evolution-cycle ceiling (F1 cycle 3 design): formal-axis = categorical-limit (sopfr(6)=5 anchors CA(5) structural minimum) + physical-axis = bekenstein-bound (info density per substrate) + computational-axis = comparison-fairness saturation (matched compute + corpus + metric = canonical comparison ceiling). 3-axis joint maintained per raw 72.

tier_1_promotions.claim: harness design spec ready, ~150 LoC impl path defined

fixpoint_marker: F1 cycle 3 design ceiling — categorical-limit (sopfr(6)=5 minimum) + bekenstein info-density + comparison-fairness saturation

obstructions.description: Design spec only this cycle. ~150 LoC impl + selftest + production runs = ~1-2 day implementation cycle separately.
atlas R28 sopfr(6)=5 anchored CA(5) but the SPECIFIC transition rule (cell update function) is NOT explicitly defined in consciousness_laws.json or atlas — rule design is open.
param-matched tiny Transformer (1280 params) is far below typical scale (transformers are billions). Comparison advantage may be parameter-regime artifact rather than architecture artifact.

honesty_triad.write_barrier: this witness JSON only. Design spec for tool, NOT actual tool impl.
no_fabrication_evidence: atlas R28 sopfr(6)=5: cited in Law 2061 N6 structural-temporal context
edu/cell + sumt_atom_factory: anima training directory existence confirmed
